/**
 * Static Tool Inventory - Generated from MCP Server Source Code
 *
 * This list is derived from the mittwald-mcp source code at:
 * src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/
 *
 * DO NOT CALL THE MCP SERVER DIRECTLY - only spawned Claude Code
 * subprocesses have MCP authentication configured.
 *
 * Total tools: 171 (excluding login_reset and login_token for security)
 */

import type { DiscoveredTool } from '../types/index.js';

/**
 * Static list of all MCP tool names
 * Format: mcp__mittwald__mittwald_<domain>_<action>
 */
export const STATIC_TOOL_NAMES: readonly string[] = [
  // App Management
  'mcp__mittwald__mittwald_app_copy',

  'mcp__mittwald__mittwald_app_versions',

  // Backup Management

  // Stack Management
  'mcp__mittwald__mittwald_stack_delete',

  // User Management
  'mcp__mittwald__mittwald_user_accessible_projects',

] as const;

/**
 * Convert static tool names to DiscoveredTool objects
 */
export function getStaticTools(): DiscoveredTool[] {
  return STATIC_TOOL_NAMES.map((name) => ({
    name,
    description: '', // Descriptions are not needed for test execution
    inputSchema: {}, // Schemas are not needed for test execution
  }));
}

/**
 * Total count of available tools
 */
export const STATIC_TOOL_COUNT = STATIC_TOOL_NAMES.length;

An exception to this is the 'User' configuration, which will be overridden by this command to either your authenticated mStudio user or the user specified with the --ssh-user flag. See https://linux.die.net/man/5/ssh_config for a reference on the configuration file. This command will also look for a file named .mw-rsync-filter in the current directory and use it as a filter file for rsync. # Mittwald CLI Coverage Matrix

Total CLI commands: 178

Covered by MCP tools: 168

Missing wrappers: 0

Status legend: ✅ Covered, ⚠️ Missing, ➖ Not Applicable

## app

| CLI Command | Description | Status | MCP Tool | Tool Definition | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| app copy | Copy an app within a project | ✅ Covered | mittwald_app_copy | src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/app/copy-cli.ts |  |
| app create node | Creates new custom Node.js installation. | ✅ Covered | mittwald_app_create_node | src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/app/create/node-cli.ts |  |

| app create python | Creates new custom python site installation. | ✅ Covered | mittwald_app_create_python | src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/app/create/python-cli.ts |  |
| app create static | Creates new custom static site installation. | ✅ Covered | mittwald_app_create_static | src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/app/create/static-cli.ts |  |
| app dependency list | Get all available dependencies | ✅ Covered | mittwald_app_dependency_list | src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/app/dependency-list-cli.ts | Provides optional filtering by dependency tags and app installation context. |
| app dependency update |  | ✅ Covered | mittwald_app_dependency_update | src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/app/dependency-update-cli.ts | Supports batch updates, quiet mode, and update policy configuration. |
| app dependency versions | Get all available versions of a particular dependency | ✅ Covered | mittwald_app_dependency_versions | src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/app/dependency-versions-cli.ts | Includes version-range filtering and recommended version highlighting. |
| app download | This command downloads the filesystem of an app installation to your local machine via rsync. For this, rsync needs to be installed on your system. This command relies on connecting to your hosting environment via SSH. For this, it will use your systems SSH client under the hood, and will respect your SSH configuration in ~/.ssh/config. An exception to this is the 'User' configuration, which will be overridden by this command to either your authenticated mStudio user or the user specified with the --ssh-user flag. See https://linux.die.net/man/5/ssh_config for a reference on the configuration file. This command will also look for a file named .mw-rsync-filter in the current directory and use it as a filter file for rsync. Have a look at https://manpages.ubuntu.com/manpages/noble/en/man1/rsync.1.html#filter%20rules for more information on how to write filter rules. | ✅ Covered | mittwald_app_download | src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/app/download-cli.ts |  |
| app exec | This command relies on connecting to your hosting environment via SSH. For this, it will use your systems SSH client under the hood, and will respect your SSH configuration in ~/.ssh/config. An exception to this is the 'User' configuration, which will be overridden by this command to either your authenticated mStudio user or the user specified with the --ssh-user flag. See https://linux.die.net/man/5/ssh_config for a reference on the configuration file. | ⚠️ Missing |  |  | Allowed missing (interactive): Interactive or streaming CLI command excluded until the MCP protocol supports bidirectional streaming; see docs/interactive-commands-decision.md for details. |

An exception to this is the 'User' configuration, which will be overridden by this command to either your authenticated mStudio user or the user specified with the --ssh-user flag. content.text:
Perfect! I now have comprehensive information. Let me create a summary report with the findings.

## Summary: mail/deliverybox/update MCP Tool

Based on my search of the codebase, here is complete information about the **mail/deliverybox/update** MCP tool:

### Tool Overview

**MCP Tool Name:** `mittwald_mail_deliverybox_update`

**Purpose:** Update a delivery box (which can be associated with a mailbox or forward to another address)

**Files:**
- Tool Definition: `/Users/robert/Code/mittwald-mcp/src/constants/tool/mittwald-cli/mail/deliverybox/update-cli.ts`
- Handler Implementation: `/Users/robert/Code/mittwald-mcp/src/handlers/tools/mittwald-cli/mail/deliverybox/update-cli.ts`

### Tool Parameters

The tool accepts the following parameters (defined in the input schema):

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|-----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `id` | string | Yes | ID of the delivery box to update |
| `description` | string | No | New description for the delivery box |
| `quiet` | boolean | No | Suppress process output and only display a machine-readable summary |
| `password` | string | No | Delivery box password (CAUTION: providing this flag may log your password in shell history) |
| `randomPassword` | boolean | No | Generate a random password |

### How It Works

1. **CLI Command Construction** (lines 13-20 of handler):
   - Builds command arguments: `mw mail deliverybox update [id] [options]`
   - Includes optional flags like `--quiet`, `--description`, `--password`, `--random-password`

2. **Execution** (lines 57-61 of handler):
   - Invokes the Mittwald CLI tool with the constructed arguments
   - Captures stdout and stderr output

3. **Output Parsing**:
   - **Quiet Mode**: Extracts the last line of output (usually machine-readable format)
   - **Random Password**: Extracts the generated password from output using regex pattern matching
   - Returns both the raw output and parsed data

4. **Error Handling** (lines 34-51 of handler):
   - Maps CLI errors to user-friendly messages
   - Handles permission denied (403), not found (404), and invalid format errors
   - Provides contextual error messages for common failure scenarios

### Response Format

The tool returns a formatted response containing:

```typescript
{
  id: string;                    // The delivery box ID
  updated: true;                 // Indicates successful update
  description?: string;          // If description was provided
  password?: string;             // If randomPassword was used
  output: string;                // Raw CLI output
}
```

### Example Usage Scenarios

1. **Update Description Only:**
   ```
   Use mittwald_mail_deliverybox_update with:
   - id: "deliverybox-123"
   - description: "New description"
   ```

2. **Generate Random Password:**
   ```
   Use mittwald_mail_deliverybox_update with:
   - id: "deliverybox-123"
   - randomPassword: true
   ```

3. **Update with Custom Password (quiet mode):**
   ```
   Use mittwald_mail_deliverybox_update with:
   - id: "deliverybox-123"
   - password: "NewPassword123"
   - quiet: true
   ```

### Security Notes

- Password parameters may be logged in shell history - users should be cautious when providing them directly
- The tool requires proper OAuth authentication via the Mittwald CLI
- Permission errors (403) indicate authentication issues or insufficient privileges

### Coverage Status

According to the documentation (`/Users/robert/Code/mittwald-mcp/docs/mittwald-cli-coverage.md`, line 190), this tool is **Covered** and maps to the CLI command `mail deliverybox update`.
